assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines available version as 'newest stable version you can manually upgrade to right now' and mentions it can be chosen for manual upgrades

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: While it correctly defines default as what GKE uses for new clusters, it doesn't explain that it's typically the same as auto-upgrade target or mention the separate promotion stages during minor version introductions

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response defines auto-upgrade target correctly but doesn't mention that it's cluster-specific based on policies - it presents it as a general timeline rather than cluster-specific determination

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response doesn't explain that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same or that they can differ during minor version rollouts - it treats them as having different purposes without explaining their typical relationship

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions, or that different clusters on the same channel can have different auto-upgrade targets

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention maintenance exclusions or 'no minor' exclusions and their impact on auto-upgrade targets

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info command or checking EoS timestamps

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response doesn't reference GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
